things.name: Red Square & the Kremlin
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage fortress at the heart of Russia with Lenin's Mausoleum and the Kremlin Palace

things.name: St Basil's Cathedral
things.description: Iconic 1561 onion-domed cathedral on Red Square, the most recognisable symbol of Russia

things.name: Moscow Metro
things.description: World's most beautiful underground railway with palatial 1930s-50s stations of marble and mosaics

things.name: Bolshoi Theatre
things.description: Russia's most famous ballet and opera theatre, rebuilt in its original Neo-Classical splendour

things.name: Tretyakov Gallery
things.description: The world's greatest collection of Russian art from icons to the Avant-Garde in a fairy-tale building

things.name: GUM Department Store
things.description: Spectacular 1893 glass-roofed arcaded shopping gallery on Red Square

things.name: Arbat Street
things.description: Moscow's bohemian pedestrian street with artists, musicians, souvenir sellers and historic cafes

things.name: Cathedral of Christ the Saviour
things.description: Russia's largest cathedral rebuilt after Stalin demolished the 1839 original to build a swimming pool

things.name: Gorky Park
things.description: Moscow's iconic riverside park transformed into a world-class public space with outdoor gym and beach

things.name: Novodevichy Convent & Cemetery
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage convent with the famous cemetery where Khrushchev and Chekhov are buried
